nodes.context: Indigenous Latino American street gang originating in South Chicago, offshoot of Gangster Disciples.
Street gang; former gang of founder Rudy Rios.
Major Chicago street gang; parent organization of Spanish Gangster Disciples.
Latino street gang; Rudy Rios was a former member.
Street gang; allied with SGDs in early 80s.
Street gang; controlled Clark & Olive area; enemy of Northside SGDs.
Folk Nation gang; ally of SGDs.
Latino street gang; ally and later rival of SGDs.
Street gang; ally of SGDs.
Latino street gang; former ally, later enemy of SGDs.
Latino street gang; ally of SGDs.
People Nation gang; rival of SGDs.
Street gang; rival of SGDs.
Folk Nation gang; took over SGD territory by 1997.
Folk Nation gang; ally of SGDs.
Street gang; ally of SGDs.
Street gang; ally of SGDs.
Street gang; former gang of Shaky.
Chicago street gang; at odds with BGDs in late 1970s.
Street gang; rival of SGDs.

edges.evidence_quote: It is an offshoot of the Gangster Disciples.
edges.context: SGD is an offshoot of Gangster Disciples.

edges.evidence_quote: The gang was founded by Rudy Rios, who was a former member of the Latin Scorpions street gang.
edges.context: Founder Rudy Rios was a former member of Latin Scorpions.

edges.evidence_quote: Rudy Rios, who was a former member of the Latin Scorpions (Maniac Latin Disciples) street gang.
edges.context: Founder Rudy Rios was a former member of Maniac Latin Disciples.

edges.evidence_quote: In the early 80s, members of the SGDs were incarcerated with members of the Insane Two One Boys, notably a Two One elder named Pepe, this brought a friendship with the two organizations that carried out to the streets.
edges.context: SGDs and Insane Two One Boys became friends through incarceration.

edges.evidence_quote: At this time the only true enemy of the Northside SGDs were the P.R. Stones street gang.
edges.context: P.R. Stones were the only enemy of Northside SGDs.

edges.evidence_quote: Drug sales created good relationships with all the Folks in the surrounding areas, including the Simon City Royals.
edges.context: SGDs had good relationships with Simon City Royals through drug sales.

edges.evidence_quote: Drug sales created good relationships with all the Folks in the surrounding areas, including the ... Imperial Gangsters.
edges.context: SGDs had good relationships with Imperial Gangsters through drug sales.

edges.evidence_quote: Drug sales created good relationships with all the Folks in the surrounding areas, including the ... Harrison Gents.
edges.context: SGDs had good relationships with Harrison Gents through drug sales.

edges.evidence_quote: Drug sales created good relationships with all the Folks in the surrounding areas, including the ... Latin Eagles.
edges.context: SGDs had good relationships with Latin Eagles through drug sales.

edges.evidence_quote: Drug sales created good relationships with all the Folks in the surrounding areas, including the ... Spanish Cobras.
edges.context: SGDs had good relationships with Spanish Cobras through drug sales.

edges.evidence_quote: In fact it was not strange to see even Latin Kings gang members come through to buy large quantities of marijuana to fund their own operations.
edges.context: Latin Kings bought marijuana from SGDs.

edges.evidence_quote: Demon also managed to wage a bloody war against the Latin Eagles street gang, one of the Spanish Gangster Disciples closest allies at one time.
edges.context: SGDs under Demon waged war against Latin Eagles, former allies.

edges.evidence_quote: Demon attempted to start a 'set' on West Hirsch Street, which was a MLD stronghold.
edges.context: SGDs under Demon encroached on MLD territory, causing conflict.

edges.evidence_quote: Demon ... had already started conflicts with the Maniac Latin Disciples and Imperial Gangsters on the streets.
edges.context: SGDs under Demon started conflicts with Imperial Gangsters.

edges.evidence_quote: Specifically due to constant feuding with local area gangs such as the Latin Counts and Latin Dragons.
edges.context: SGDs feuded with Latin Counts.

edges.evidence_quote: Specifically due to constant feuding with local area gangs such as the Latin Counts and Latin Dragons.
edges.context: SGDs feuded with Latin Dragons.

edges.evidence_quote: the Almighty Ambrose, another well known Folk Nation gang, took over most their former territory at 87th & Houston by 1997.
edges.context: Almighty Ambrose took over SGD territory by 1997.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: most of Folk Nation, Gangster Disciples, Satan Disciples, Insane Two One Boys Of 21st And Leavitt, Gangster Two Six, Vato Loko Boyz
edges.context: Satan Disciples are listed as allies in infobox.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: most of Folk Nation, Gangster Disciples, Satan Disciples, Insane Two One Boys Of 21st And Leavitt, Gangster Two Six, Vato Loko Boyz
edges.context: Gangster Two Six is listed as ally in infobox.

edges.context: Vato Loko Boyz is listed as ally in infobox.

edges.context: Latin Kings listed as rival in infobox.
